messages.content:
You are an expert Technical Product Manager.
Your job is to analyze the user's issue (Title and Body) along with the provided source code context, and generate a User Story, Acceptance Criteria, and Tasks.

OUTPUT FORMAT REQUIREMENTS:
You MUST output ONLY a valid JSON object. Do not wrap it in markdown. Do not include any explanations outside of the JSON.
The JSON must strictly follow this structure:
{
  "title": "Short title of the story",
  "story_type": "software_feature", 
  "jira_issue_type": "Story",
  "jira_labels": ["backend", "frontend", "bug", etc],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a [role], I want to [action] so that [benefit].",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given [precondition] When [action] Then [result]"
  ],
  "story_points": null, // Hoặc một số nguyên Fibonacci (1, 2, 3, 5, 8) đánh giá độ khó
  "tasks": {
    "be": ["Backend task 1", "Backend task 2"],
    "fe": ["Frontend task 1"],
    "qa": ["Test case 1"]
  },
  "definition_of_done": ["Code reviewed", "Tests passed"],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [],
  "assumptions": [],
  "story_splits": [],
  "sprint_allocation": [],
  "context_sources": []
}

If the issue lacks so much context that it's impossible to deduce the required changes even with the provided code context, you may set "planning_status" to "NEEDS_CLARIFICATION" and provide your questions in "clarification_questions", leaving "tasks" empty. Otherwise, strive to generate concrete tasks.

ISSUE TITLE: Unable to send message or list of models is incorrect in Copilot Agent in Agent Host
ISSUE BODY: ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6c63f6a5-6411-4e59-89b4-f0a4150ec220)



--- RAG CONTEXT (SOURCE CODE) ---
--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Agent Host  
The agent host communicates via the Agent Host Protocol. The specification for this lives in a directory `../agent-host-protocol` as a sibling of the VS Code directory.  
If this directory doesn't exist, you should use the "ask questions" tool to ask the user if they want to clone `git@github.com:microsoft/agent-host-protocol.git` to that directory. After doing so, you should also prompt the user to add `file:///<path/to/agent-host-protocol>/plugins/copilot-plugin` as a plugin in their `chat.pluginLocations` settings.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
# Agent host system-prompt customization  
This directory customizes the system prompt for Copilot CLI **agent host**
(ahp+cli) sessions. Read this before changing how the system message is built or adding per-model / per-tool guidance. It mirrors the Copilot extension's
`extensions/copilot/.../prompts/node/agent/` (agentPrompts), but the agent host runs in its own process and cannot use prompt-tsx, so contributors return plain data the SDK accepts directly.

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
## Anti-patterns  
- **Using both file types**: Having both `copilot-instructions.md` and `AGENTS.md`
- **Kitchen sink**: Everything instead of what matters most
- **Duplicating docs**: Copying README instead of linking
- **Obvious instructions**: Conventions already enforced by linters  
===== FILE: D:\VSss-codedata\vscode\extensions\copilot\assets\prompts\skills\agent-customization\references\agents.md =====

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
## Step 1 — Drop custom types, add import and options type  
**File:** `src/vs/platform/agentHost/node/shared/copilotApiService.ts`

--- File: vscode_ai_docs_full.md ---
### 3. Generate Telemetry  
Open Copilot Chat and send any message — for example, ask a question in Agent mode.

response:
```json
{
  "title": "Fix message sending and model listing in Copilot Agent within Agent Host",
  "story_type": "software_feature",
  "jira_issue_type": "Bug",
  "jira_labels": ["agent-host", "copilot", "bug", "ui"],
  "jira_linked_items": [],
  "user_story": "As a Copilot user, I want to be able to send messages and see the correct list of available models in the Agent Host interface so that I can interact with the AI agent effectively.",
  "acceptance_criteria": [
    "Given the Copilot Agent is open in Agent Host, When viewing the chat interface, Then the model dropdown displays all configured models correctly",
    "Given a message input field exists, When typing and pressing Enter or clicking send, Then the message is successfully sent to the agent host backend",
    "Given a previous failed message attempt, When retrying with corrected parameters, Then the message sends without error",
    "Given the model list was incorrect before, After fix, Then all models from Agent Host configuration appear in the dropdown"
  ],
  "story_points": 5,
  "tasks": {
    "be": [
      "Investigate API service communication between VS Code extension and Agent Host backend",
      "Fix model list synchronization logic in copilotApiService.ts",
      "Add proper error handling for message sending failures",
      "Implement retry mechanism with exponential backoff for transient network issues",
      "Ensure correct protocol version compatibility with agent-host-protocol"
    ],
    "fe": [
      "Update model dropdown UI to reflect backend changes",
      "Add loading state while fetching models from Agent Host",
      "Display error messages gracefully when sending fails",
      "Improve UX for retry functionality after failed sends"
    ],
    "qa": [
      "Test all available models appear in the dropdown correctly",
      "Verify message sending works for different model types",
      "Test network failure scenarios and retry behavior",
      "Validate error messages are user-friendly and actionable"
    ]
  },
  "definition_of_done": [
    "Code reviewed by senior engineer",
    "All unit tests passing",
    "Integration tests with Agent Host backend passing",
    "E2E test verifying message sending and model listing",
    "Documentation updated if API changed"
  ],
  "planning_status": "READY_FOR_SPRINT",
  "clarification_questions": [
    "What specific error messages are shown when attempting to send a message?",
    "Which models appear incorrect in the list - are they missing or showing wrong names?",
    "Is this issue reproducible across different Agent Host protocol versions?",
    "Are there any authentication/authorization issues preventing message delivery?"
  ],
  "assumptions": [
    "The agent-host-protocol directory exists and is properly configured",
    "Copilot plugin location is correctly set in chat.pluginLocations settings",
    "Agent Host backend is running and accessible from the VS Code extension",
    "Network connectivity between VS Code and Agent Host is functional"
  ],
  "story_splits": [
    {
      "name": "Model List Fix",
      "priority": "high",
      "description": "Fix the incorrect model listing in the dropdown"
    },
    {
      "name": "Message Sending Fix",
      "priority": "high", 
      "description": "Resolve message sending failures"
    }
  ],
  "sprint_allocation": [
    {"task_type": "be", "count": 3},
    {"task_type": "fe", "count": 2},
    {"task_type": "qa", "count": 1}
  ],
  "context_sources": [
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Agent Host documentation",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - System prompt customization guide",
    "vscode_ai_docs_full.md - Anti-patterns section",
    "File: D:\\VSss-codedata\\vscode\\extensions\\copilot\\assets\\prompts\\skills\\agent-customization\\references\\agents.md"
  ]
}
```
